title: Conduit Routing and Bend Lab
summary: Learners practice selecting conduit type, laying out runs, placing supports, and making common bends while keeping fill, protection, and installation discipline in view.
learningObjectives: Recognize common conduit systems such as EMT, PVC, and flexible raceway.
Plan simple runs using couplings, connectors, offsets, and 90-degree bends.
Connect raceway choices to conductor protection and installation quality.

environment.setting: Shop-style conduit bay with wall runs, obstacle posts, bending station, and material rack
environment.focus: Foundational raceway planning for apprentice installations

interactables.label: Conduit type rack
interactables.teaches: Compares EMT, PVC, and flexible raceway based on application and environment.

interactables.label: Hand bender station
interactables.teaches: Introduces stub-up, offset, and 90-degree bend intent.

interactables.label: Route layout guide
interactables.teaches: Shows how to avoid obstacles and maintain clean routing.

interactables.label: Support placement overlay
interactables.teaches: Highlights where the run still needs secure support and finishing hardware.

tasks: Choose the conduit type that best fits each environment shown.
Lay out a wall run around an obstruction using an offset.
Place connectors, couplings, and supports in the correct order.
Inspect the finished run for clean alignment and missing hardware.
completion: Learner selects suitable raceway for the scenario.
Learner completes a clean routed conduit path.
Learner passes the final installation inspection check.
